columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: The date and time of the IPS detection event, expressed as a UTC-formatted string.

columns.name: Identities
columns.description: All tunnel identities that are associated with this request.

columns.name: IdentityTypes
columns.description: The type of identity that is associated with this request.

columns.name: GeneratorId
columns.description: Unique ID assigned to the part of the IPS that generated the event.

columns.name: SignatureId
columns.description: Used to uniquely identify signatures.

columns.name: SignatureMessage
columns.description: A brief description of the signature.

columns.name: SignatureListId
columns.description: Unique ID assigned to a Default or Custom Signature List.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: The severity level of the rule. Valid values are: High, Medium, Low, and Very Low.

columns.name: AttackClassification
columns.description: The category of attack detected by a rule that is part of a more general type of attack class. Valid values are: trojan-activity, attempted-user, and unknown.

columns.name: Cves
columns.description: A list of information about security vulnerabilities and exposures.

columns.name: IpProtocol
columns.description: The actual protocol of the traffic, such as TCP, UDP, ICMP.

columns.name: SessionId
columns.description: The unique identifier of a session, which is used to group the correlated events between various services.

columns.name: SourceIp
columns.description: The IP of the computer making the request.

columns.name: SourcePort
columns.description: The port number of the request.

columns.name: DestinationIp
columns.description: The IP address of the destination.

columns.name: DestinationPort
columns.description: The destination port number of the request.

columns.name: Action
columns.description: The action performed when criteria meets a rule, for example: block, warn, and would_block.

columns.name: OperationMode
columns.description: The mode of operation of the IPS, either detection or prevention. Valid values are: IDS, IPS, and UNKNOWN.

columns.name: PolicyResourceId
columns.description: The ID of the IPS policy resource. An example of a policy resource is: signature list.

columns.name: Direction
columns.description: The direction of the packet that matches the signature. Valid values are: S2C, C2S, and UNKNOWN.

columns.name: FirewallRuleId
columns.description: The ID of the rule that matches the firewall session.

columns.name: IpsConfigType
columns.description: The type of the IPS configuration. Valid values are: CONFIG, PROFILE, and UNKNOWN.

columns.name: AwsRegion
columns.description: The AWS region where Secure Access stores your logs.

columns.name: ApplicationId
columns.description: The ID of the destination application.

columns.name: CasiCategoryIds
columns.description: The name of the Application category to which the App ID belongs.

columns.name: DataCenter
columns.description: The name of the data center that processed the user-generated traffic.

columns.name: OrganizationId
columns.description: The Secure Access organization ID.

columns.name: EgressIp
columns.description: The public IP address assigned to a session as it exits the Secure Access ZTA infrastructure en route to the destination application.

columns.name: Egress
columns.description: TRUE indicates that the egress IP was a reserved IP.

columns.name: EnforcedBy
columns.description: The Secure Access component or service that enforced the policy or control related to this event (e.g., Firewall, Web Proxy).

columns.name: FtdEnforcementId
columns.description: The unique identifier of the enforcement action taken by a Firepower Threat Defense (FTD) device integrated with Secure Access.

columns.name: FtdEnforcementName
columns.description: The name or type of enforcement action taken by a FTD device integrated with Secure Access (e.g., Malware Block, URL Category Block).

columns.name: EventCorrelationId
columns.description: A unique identifier generated for each network request, the Event Correlation ID stitches together all related events across various security services (Firewall, SWG, ZTNA) to provide a unified, end-to-end view of a single traffic flow.
